title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Philip suggests that Mary travel to Sunny Corner to distract herself, noting that her father is easy to get along with. Mary feels a sense of futile anger while reflecting on her mother's influence and her own death. Meanwhile, Leo Aghart reflects on the situation involving Gwendolyn and Inspector Hueish. He recalls comforting Rachel after she reported Jacques's reckless behavior and threats. Gwendolyn leaves the house unnoticed after gathering letters; Leo notes that both he and Gwendolyn had the opportunity and motive to be involved in the case because they were in love. Nearby, Gwendolyn lies awake harboring hatred for Rachel Ackerley, while a voice seemingly warns her in the darkness that she will never have her husband. Hester experiences a dream where Donald Craig leaves her at an abyss, only for Arthur Calgary to reach out and help her. Tina lies awake reflecting on her love for the deceased Mrs. Aigle.

Inspector Hueish arrives to re-examine the case, admitting the police made a mistake in arresting Jacques because they could not verify his story regarding hitchhiking. While Hester expresses skepticism that the killer will ever be found, Hueish insists on his persistence. Leo Aghart provides a statement, recounting that after tea at five o'clock, he returned to the study to read while his wife, Mrs. Aigle, went to her office. They heard a doorbell but did not know who it was. Leo estimates that approximately 45 minutes later, Mrs. Aigle entered the study to report that Jacques had been with her, acting rude and demanding money to avoid jail, which she had refused to provide.
